data.title: Top quant-rated global financial stocks: KINS, ROOT, SUPV
data.description:
Global financials continue to head the triple momentum ranking, according to BofA Securities.

Triple momentum encompasses earnings momentum, price momentum, and news momentum.

Analysts explain that earnings momentum monitors the changing consensus of earnings estimates and is defined “as the three-month change in the consensus EPS estimate for each stock.”

Price Momentum is the sentiment of market participants buying and selling equities and is defined “as the slope of the log-linear regression using daily prices over the preceding 12-months.”

Finally, news momentum captures “the sentiment from all other market players including journalists, experts, regulators, corporates, and more.”

The global sectors ranking highest in triple momentum are insurance (KIE), diversified financials (VFH), and banks (KBE).

Here are the highest-ranking global stocks in insurance, diversified financials, and banks, according to Seeking Alpha quant metrics:

  • Kingstone Companies Inc. (KINS) – Quant rating: 4.99

data.title: Lion Electric surges after getting a temporary reprieve from its creditors
data.description:
Canadian electric truck and bus maker Lion Electric (NYSE:LEV) jumped 55% after winning a temporary reprieve from its creditors. The company also said it would lay off 400 of its employees in Canada and the U.S.

Lion Electric also announced that it will suspend manufacturing operations at its facility located in Joliet, Illinois, as a result of the workforce reduction, according to a statement on Monday.

Separately, Bloomberg reported that Mach Capital, the investment unit of Canadian real estate developer Group Mach, is in talks to provide funding to Lion Electric (LEV). Mach will only invest if members of Montreal's Saputo family are also willing to give more cash to try to save the EV maker, a person familiar told Bloomberg.

Mach’s interest was reported earlier by the Journal de Montreal.

data.title: Nvidia competitor Tensorrent raises $693M in Series D funding round
data.description:
• Canadian startup Tenstorrent has raised $693M in a new Series D funding round as it looks to compete with Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and others in the artificial intelligence accelerator market.
  • Tensorrent, which chose Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) last year to build its AI chips, raised funding from Samsung, Amazon co-founder Jeff Bezos and a host of others. Law firm Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati and investment firm Barclays helped with the funding round, Tensorrent Chief Operating Officer Keith Witek said.
  • The new funding values Tensorrent at $2.6B, Bloomberg reported.
  • Some of Tenstorrent's chips use RISC-V technology, an alternative to Arm (ARM) -based architecture that is being championed by Qualcomm (QCOM) and some other semiconductor companies. The other major semiconductor architecture is x86, which is championed by Intel (INTC) and AMD (AMD).

data.title: Fed's Waller leans toward December rate cut, sees more cuts over next year
data.description:
• Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller expects rate cuts to continue over the next year "until we approach a more neutral setting of the policy rate," he said in prepared text for a speech at the American Institute for Economic Research on Monday.
  • Furthermore, he said he leans toward supporting a cut at the Federal Open Market Committee's December meeting based on economic data at hand today.
  • He said forecasts still show that inflation will continue toward the Fed's 2% goal over the medium term.
  • Breaking... check back for updates.

data.title: NextEra Partners powers higher as Morgan Stanley double upgrades
data.description:
NextEra Energy Partners (NYSE:NEP) +13.6% in Monday's trading after Morgan Stanley double upgrades the clean energy company to Overweight from Underweight with a $22 price target, saying the stock has overly discounted negative outcomes of its ongoing strategic review.

"We see an attractive entry point for NEP in the recent selloff to position ahead of a potential near-term catalyst: the resolution of its strategic review, perhaps as soon as December or January," Morgan Stanley's Robert Kad writes.

The analyst says NextEra Partners (NEP) now trades below its intrinsic value across likely outcomes of the strategic review following the stock's 17% decline since reporting a surprise Q3 net loss, while he currently models a 50% distribution cut in Q4, an expectation he says is now more than adequately reflected in the stock price.

More broadly, "demand for renewables over the long-term is more durable than is currently appreciated by the market," according to Kad, saying that as demand for energy rises, given drivers such as data center growth and penetration of electric vehicles, "even in the absence of tax credits, there will still be demand for renewables, albeit potentially at a more modest level, given the underlying power demand trends."

Kad maintains Buy ratings for Clearway Energy (CWEN) and HA Sustainable Infrastructure (HASI), which he says will remain well positioned to capture growth in contracted renewable investments but may trade sideways awaiting clarity on potential revisions to the Inflation Reduction Act.

data.title: Black Friday winners & losers: Sell side weighs in on Black Friday sales results
data.description:
The results are in, and retailers can breathe a sigh of relief as Black Friday tallies point to strong start to the official holiday spending season.

Wall Street analysts weigh in on the significance of data compiled by Adobe Analytics, Mastercard (NYSE:MA) and Salesforce (NYSE:CRM), and what trends will translate into opportunities for investors.

Brick and mortar vs online: After a spike in online shopping during the pandemic, consumers continue to prefer this channel for its convenience and ease of price comparisons. Adobe Analytics showed a 10% increase in on-line shopping on Black Friday reaching a record high of $10.8B. There is also stronger demand by younger consumers with purchases made from smartphones outpacing computer sales.

Beauty: Data compiled by Canaccord Genuity showed that retailers don’t have to “irrationally promote” within the category to attract young shoppers. Ulta (NASDAQ:ULTA) got an early jump on holiday spending with promotions that kicked off back in late-October. Deals spanned across all categories but seemed heaviest in skin care and makeup. Piper Sandler’s Korinne Wolfmeyer is more cautious on Ulta (ULTA), however, and sees a “still challenged retailer” with margin pressure likely in the near/intermediate term “given what we’re seeing this holiday season so far." Wolfmeyer favors e.l.f. Beauty (NYSE:ELF) and Interparfums (NASDAQ:IPAR) instead.

Footwear and Apparel: Hoka (NYSE:DECK) and On (NYSE:ONON) were top sellers in the footwear category this Black Friday given the high level of digital engagement, Needham’s Tom Nikic said, noting the improved promotional activity at Foot Locker (NYSE:FL), UGG (DECK), and Under Armour (UA, UAA) as well as Hoka (DECK). In contrast, Nikic saw lackluster promotional activity for Crocs’ Hey Dude (NASDAQ:CROX), Lululemon (NASDAQ:LULU), Nike (NYSE:NKE), Skechers (NYSE:SKX), and Steve Madden (NASDAQ:SHOO).

Toys and Leisure: As the reigning number one item for another year, BofA Securities calls out Hot Wheels (NASDAQ:MAT) and Barbie (MAT), Play Doh (NASDAQ:HAS), Transformers (HAS), and Monopoly (HAS) as this season’s top selling toys. Alternatively, the packaging snafu for Wicked dolls (MAT) kept merchandise off the shelves for the busiest shopping day of the year.

Outside of toys, Yeti (NYSE:YETI), once a popular gift item in years past, is beginning to lose traction to Stanley (NYSE:SWK) drinkwear, sold at competitive prices to Yeti (YETI).

“We believe Stanley had the largest shelf space allocated in drinkware at both [DICK’s Sporting Goods and Academy Sports & Outdoors] and was featured in the front of store/power aisle at both retailers.

Related Tickers: Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), Best Buy (NYSE:BBY), Walmart (NYSE:WMT), Target (NYSE:TGT), JAKKS Pacific (NASDAQ:JAKK), GameStop (NYSE:GME), Abercrombie & Fitch (NYSE:ANF), Gap (NYSE:GAP), Victoria’s Secret (NYSE:VSCO), Ross Stores (NASDAQ:ROST), TJX Companies (NYSE:TJX), Bath & Body Works (NYSE:BBWI), Five Below (NASDAQ:FIVE), Burlington Stores (NYSE:BURL), Urban Outfitters (NASDAQ:URBN), SPDR S&P Retail ETF (NYSEARCA:XRT), Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLY).

data.title: Sleep Number holder Stadium Capital nominates four for board (update)
data.description:
Update 3:15pm: Adds Sleep Number response.Sleep Number's (NASDAQ:SNBR) largest shareholder Stadium Capital nominated four directors amid a proxy fight with the mattress retailer.

Stadium Capital, which is Sleep Number's (SNBR) largest holder with an 11.7% stake, nominated Patrick A. Hopf, Jeffrey T. Jackson, Jessica M. Prager and Kevin Baker to the company's board, according to a letter sent to SBNR's board on Monday.

The letter comes after Stadium a week ago threatened a new proxy fight with the mattress retailer. Stadium said it wanted an executive chairman appointed to run the company as CEO Shelly Ibach exits the company, which Sleep Number (SNBR) announced late last month.

"We did not come to this decision lightly, as we strongly prefer private engagement with the management teams and boards of our portfolio companies on ideas for value creation and improved governance," Stadium Capital wrote in the letter.

Stadium last September threatened a proxy fight and then came to an agreement with Sleep Number (SNBR) to add two directors to the board.

"We strongly believe it is in shareholders’ best interest to support the Board at this critical time," Sleep Number said in a statement emailed to Seeking Alpha on Monday. "There is no pretext for Stadium’s disruptive action, and we are disappointed that Stadium has taken this approach. Stadium has not provided any CEO candidates or input on the profile. "

"Nevertheless, the Board will carefully review Stadium’s director candidates and expects to interview them," Sleep Number continued in its response. "In the meantime, the Board remains focused on identifying and recruiting the optimal CEO and overseeing the Company’s continued business transformation."

Shares of Sleep Number (SNBR) rose 1.7% on Monday.

data.title: Merus rises 10% following updated phase 2 data on petosemtamab for head and neck cancer
data.description:
• Merus (NASDAQ:MRUS) is up ~10% in Monday trading after releasing updated data from a phase 2 trial on petosemtamab as a second-line+ treatment for those with head and neck squamous cell carcinoma.
  • As of a March 6, 2024, cutoff, 42 patients were randomized to the 1500 mg and 1100 mg doses. At 1500 mg, 12 were evaluable for response. Five esponses were observed including 1 complete response, 3 partial responses, and 1 unconfirmed partial response that was confirmed post cutoff.
  • At 1100 mg, 10 patients were evaluable with one confirmed partial response observed.
  • Also, no grade 5 treatment emergent adverse events were reported.
  • Separately, Merus also announced it signed an agreement with privately held Partner Therapeutics, giving the latter an exclusive right to commercialize zenocutuzumab, currently in phase 1/2, for the treatment of NRG1 fusion-positive (NRG1+) cancer in the U.S.

data.title: Apple faces employee lawsuit under updated California act: report
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) faces a civil suit filed by a former employee accusing the company of silencing employees and spying on its workers.

The suit, filed Sunday in California state court by Amar Bhakta, who currently works in digital advertising for Apple, alleges employees are prohibited from discussing working conditions. It goes further, alleging employees are required to allow the tech giant to "engage in physical, video and electronic surveillance," even when they are home and off work, according to a report by Semafor.

"Apple's surveillance policies and practices chill, and thus also unlawfully restrain, employee whistleblowing, competition, freedom of employee movement in the job market, and freedom of speech," the lawsuit said, according to a report by Reuters.

Bhakta is represented by Chris Baker of Baker Dolinko & Schwartz, and Jahan Sagafi, of Outten & Golden. According to Bhakta's LinkedIn page, he was an ad operations manager at Hulu for three years before joining Apple in 2020.

The lawsuit was filed under a newly updated version of California's Private Attorneys General Act, or PAGA. PAGA makes it easier for employees to sue employers on behalf of the state. First established in 2004, it was updated this year by designating 65% of any penalties won in a lawsuit to go to the California Labor and Workforce Development Agency, while 35% goes to aggrieved employees. Previously, 75% went to the state and 25% to employees.

Apple is well known for its strong belief in privacy. Its delayed entrance into the artificial intelligence race was due in part to developing Apple Intelligence, which could be powered on-device rather than in the cloud. The iPhone is also regularly labeled as the most difficult smartphone to hack.
